32.reason: [This increment causes the loop to skip the character after '-', which may be part of another pattern or needed for output. Also, when multiple conditions match (e.g., overlapping ranges), multiple expansions may occur incorrectly. More critically, accessing str[i-1] when i=0 leads to out-of-bounds read.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.125 , reason:  this i++ combined with for loop increment causes double increment and does not handle cases where no expansion occurs (should print '-' and move)
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  Double increment of loop variable i (also incremented by for loop), causing skipping of characters after dash.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.125 , reason:  In a `for` loop, `i` is automatically incremented; this extra increment skips processing `str[i+1]`. Additionally, missing `else` branch to print '-' when expansion conditions fail.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.125 , reason:  Unnecessary increment causes skipping the character after '-' due to double increment with the for loop, and fails to print '-' when expansion conditions are not met
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This increment causes the loop to skip the character after '-', which may be part of another pattern or needed for output. Also, when multiple conditions match (e.g., overlapping ranges), multiple expansions may occur incorrectly. More critically, accessing str[i-1] when i=0 leads to out-of-bounds read.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This increment causes the loop to skip the next character after '-', which may be part of another pattern or a normal character. Also, when expansion occurs, the character after '-' should not be printed again, but the current logic doesn't prevent that because the main loop will not process it due to this extra i++. Moreover, if none of the conditions match, the '-' is not printed at all, violating the requirement to output unchanged in such cases.]
